channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Addressing Systemic Failures Following the Death of Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs
item.description: The tragic death of Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs has cast a harsh light on long-standing issues within the town camps, highlighting systemic failures in housing, child protection, and domest...

item.title: Alex Eala Advances to Round 2 at the Italian Open After Hard-Fought Match Against Fręch
item.description: Filipino tennis prodigy Alex Eala showcased her resilience and skill at the 2026 Italian Open by defeating Poland's Magdalena Fręch to secure a spot in the second round of the prestigious tournament. ...

item.title: San Miguel Beermen clinch quarterfinals berth with dominant win over Terrafirma Dyip
item.description: San Miguel Beermen have officially sealed their place in the PBA Commissioner's Cup quarterfinals after a convincing victory against Terrafirma Dyip. The match, held on May 6, 2026, showcased San Migu...

item.title: Debate over Light Rail Stage 2B and public transport alternatives in Canberra
item.description: A series of letters to The Canberra Times discuss various issues surrounding Canberra's transport policies, particularly the Stage 2B light rail project. Citizens raise concerns about fare evasion on ...

item.title: Governor Fubara Appreciates Labour Unions for Promoting Workplace Harmony in Rivers
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Sir Siminalayi Fubara, don hail Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC), Trade Union Congress (TUC) and all their affiliate unions for choosing dialogue over wahala when dey dey handle in...

item.title: Gombe magistrate sentenced for bribery with option to pay fine
item.description: The Gombe State High Court don sentence one magistrate, Mohammad Kumo, to two years and six months imprisonment for bribery. Judge H.H. Kereng give am option to pay N250,000 instead of serving the jai...

item.title: KUCCPS Sets Second Window for University and College Application Revisions
item.description: The Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has confirmed that the portal for 2025/2026 applicants will be reopened for a second application revision from May 16 to May 22. ...

item.title: Exploring the Rise in Concert and Tour Cancellations Among Top Artists
item.description: Recent months have seen a noticeable increase in concert and tour cancellations among major artists, prompting industry observers and fans to seek explanations. While various logistical and financial ...

item.title: Harry Richie Steps Back from Music Due to Low Earnings
item.description: Kenyan musician Harry Richie, widely known for his hit song 'Vaida', has declared that he is stepping away from the music industry citing financial challenges. In an interview with Oga Obinna, Richie ...

item.title: 14-Year-Old Faces Multiple Charges After Saskatoon Vehicle Collision
item.description: A 14-year-old boy has been charged with dangerous driving causing bodily harm following a fiery collision near Idylwyld Drive in Saskatoon on Tuesday. The incident involved a stolen Dodge SUV, reporte...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Hawks’ Maj-Gen Senona under scrutiny over missing R200m drugs
item.description: Earlier this year, Maj-Gen Lesetja Senona, head of the KwaZulu-Natal Hawks, testified at the Madlanga commission regarding the disappearance of R200 million worth of drugs, believed to be cocaine. The...

item.title: Microsoft faces challenges meeting clean energy targets amid rapid AI data center growth
item.description: Microsoft is facing an internal dilemma as its rapid expansion of AI data centers challenges its ambitious clean energy goals. The company has publicly committed to matching 100% of its hourly energy ...

item.title: Rabid beaver attack in New Jersey leads to multiple exposures and health warnings
item.description: Health officials in Mahwah, New Jersey, have confirmed that a beaver involved in multiple human encounters at Lake Henry tested positive for rabies after an attack reported on May 3. According to the ...

item.title: Hasan Piker Critiques AI and Shares His Daily Streaming Routine

item.title: Over 500 Terror Suspects Set for Trial in Borno After Large-Scale Investigation
item.description: Authorities for Giwa Barracks Joint Investigation Centre for Maiduguri don complete investigation of about 1,450 suspected terrorists wey security forces arrest during counter-insurgency operations. A...

item.title: Two UK Residents Self-Isolating Following Hantavirus Cases Linked to Cruise Ship
item.description: Two individuals who recently returned to the United Kingdom from the MV Hondius cruise ship have been instructed to self-isolate following a hantavirus outbreak on board, the UK Health Security Agency...

item.title: Most ASEAN Leaders Expected at 2026 Summit, Myanmar Likely Absent
item.description: The upcoming ASEAN Leaders' Summit in 2026 is set to see the attendance of heads of state from all member countries, with the notable exception of Myanmar. Officials from the ASEAN Secretariat have co...

item.title: Authorities advise parents to reinforce hand hygiene after hepatitis A cases in South Wales
item.description: Following a recent outbreak of hepatitis A in South Wales, health authorities have advised parents to pay close attention to their children's handwashing habits. The outbreak has prompted concern due ...

item.title: Exceptionally Preserved 240-Million-Year-Old Amphibian Found in Australian Garden Wall
item.description: A fossil hidden in a garden retaining wall for decades has been identified as Arenaerpeton supinatus, a 240-million-year-old amphibian. Unearthed in the 1990s by a retired chicken farmer who used rock...

item.title: Barbie Imperial recalls experiences with her “Blood vs Duty” co-stars
item.description: Barbie Imperial recently opened up about her experiences working on the TV series “Blood vs Duty,” sharing fond memories with her co-stars. In interviews, she expressed gratitude for the camaraderie a...
